items.name: Performative-UI
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Performative-UI – a react component library of design tropes
items.summary: React component library that parodies design tropes without hiding the joke.
items.why_included: HN commenters called it hilarious, well made, and useful in some contexts.
items.evidence: Comments praised the repo link, said it was “hilarious and extremely well made,” and noted that some pieces are appropriate to use in real projects.

items.name: Intuned
items.source_story_title: Launch HN: Intuned (YC S22) – Build and run reliable browser automations as code
items.summary: Browser-automation platform for reliable scripted workflows and captcha-heavy integrations.
items.why_included: The discussion stayed practical and positive around browser automation, auth, and bot handling.
items.evidence: Replies called it “Really cool!” and “Love this,” while other comments dug into auth, captcha, and browser automation tradeoffs instead of dismissing the product.

items.name: Gitdot
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Gitdot – a better GitHub. Open-source, anti-AI, and written in Rust
items.summary: Rust-based GitHub alternative with a minimalist browser-first interface.
items.why_included: Commenters reacted positively to the UI and treated it as a fresh take on GitHub browsing.
items.evidence: One comment called the product interesting, another said it had a “minimalist UI,” and the thread framed it as a real opportunity to build something fresh.

items.name: Pyrefly
items.source_story_title: Are you expected to run five Python type-checkers now?
items.summary: Fast Python type checker and language server from Meta.
items.why_included: The thread put Pyrefly directly in the Python type-checking conversation with real user interest.
items.evidence: A commenter explicitly asked for impressions of Pyrefly, and the replies weighed its place against mypy, pyright, and strict typing in Python.

items.name: MiMo-v2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed
items.source_story_title: MiMo-v2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed: 1T model with 1000 tokens per second
items.summary: Xiaomi model release focused on unusually high token throughput.
items.why_included: The speed claim drew the thread’s attention and sparked benchmarking comparisons.
items.evidence: Comments compared it with DeepSeek and stayed focused on whether the speed claims are real, which made the launch stand out from generic model chatter.

items.name: MusicDecoy
items.source_story_title: Stop the Apple Music app from launching
items.summary: Tiny Mac utility that blocks Apple Music from auto-launching.
items.why_included: The thread surfaced a real annoyance people had already been hacking around.
items.evidence: A commenter linked the MusicDecoy source directly, while others shared noTunes and Shortcut workarounds for the same Apple Music annoyance.

items.name: The Cypherpunk Library
items.summary: Online library of cypherpunk and crypto-adjacent books and texts.
items.why_included: Commenters engaged with the site as a curated library, not just a landing page.
items.evidence: Replies called the page “nice work” and “interesting,” with several comments discussing the collection, layout, and mission rather than ignoring the project.

items.name: 1worldflag
items.source_story_title: 1worldflag: A blue dot on a transparent background
items.summary: One-world flag concept built around a transparent blue-dot motif.
items.why_included: The comments treated the idea seriously enough to debate the design choice itself.
items.evidence: Commenters said the transparency was novel, said they loved it, and remarked that transparent flags have not been used more.
